label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out how many penguins are more than 5 years old and weigh more than 12 kg. The data is given in a table, so I should probably go through each penguin one by one. First, let me list out all the penguins with their details: 1. Louis: age 7, height 50 cm, weight 11 kg 2. Bernard: age 5, height 80 cm, weight 13 kg 3. Vincent: age 9, height 60 cm, weight 11 kg 4. Gwen: age 8, height 70 cm, weight 15 kg The question is asking for penguins that are more than 5 years old AND weigh more than 12 kg. So both conditions need to be true for each penguin. Let me check each one: - Louis: Age is 7, which is more than 5. Weight is 11 kg, which is less than 12. So he doesn't meet the second condition. So Louis is out. - Bernard: Age is 5. Wait, the condition is more than 5, not 5 or more. So 5 is not more than 5. So Bernard's age is exactly 5, which doesn't satisfy the first condition. Even though his weight is 13 kg (which is more than 12), since his age isn't over 5, he doesn't count. - Vincent: Age is 9, which is more than 5. Weight is 11 kg, which is less than 12. So same as Louis. Doesn't meet the weight requirement. - Gwen: Age is 8 (more than 5) and weight is 15 kg (more than 12). Both conditions satisfied. So Gwen counts. So looking at all four penguins, only Gwen meets both criteria. So the answer should be 1, which is option A. Wait, but let me double-check. Did I miss someone? The options go up to 5, but there are only four penguins listed. So the maximum possible is 4. But according to my analysis, only Gwen qualifies. Let me check again. Louis: Age 7 (>5) but weight 11 (<=12). So no. Bernard: Age 5 (not >5), weight 13. But age isn't over 5. So no. Vincent: Age 9 (>5), weight 11 (<=12). No. Gwen: Age 8 (>5), weight 15
